Explanation: Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the AUTHORS table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and cause performance issues. Explicitly list only the columns needed for AuthorDTO, e.g., select(AUTHORS.ID, AUTHORS.NAME).

Code fragment: public final TableField<AuthorsRecord, Integer> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is generically named 'id' instead of a more descriptive name like 'author_id'. Consider renaming the column to 'author_id' to make it self-documenting and avoid ambiguity when joining tables.
